title: Game News Round-Up
summary: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 14 August 2024.

items.title: **Helldivers 2** studio reveals 60-day plan of action following recent patch outcry
bullets: Fans are complaining about the nerfing of key weapons... again.

This follows an established cycle now for Helldivers 2 where a new content drop comes out, nerfs the most popular weapons, receives player base outcry, promises to do better next time and the cycle repeats.

items.title: **PlayStation** lays out **Concord** live-service roadmap, commits to at least three seasons
bullets: Season 1's launch in October will add a new map, character, story scenes and more. Season 2 will bring another new map, character and set of story scenes, plus a fresh mode. Season 3 - for now - remains under wraps.

items.title: **WayForward** is turning **Halloween** and **Ash vs Evil Dead** into 16-bit style platformers
bullets: RetroRealms: Halloween and RetroRealms: Ash vs Evil Dead are action platform games created in a 16- bit style.
Each game will cost $24.99 digitally, or can be purchased physically as a two-in-one release for $49.99.
Boss Team Games says that if players buy both games, it will give them access to new features, enabling them to play as Michael Myers or Ash in each other’s worlds.

items.title: **Alien: Romulus** pays tribute to Alien Isolation with a telephone easter egg, director says
bullets: In Alien Isolation, there is a normally a telephone save point before "scary encounters". The film pays tribute to that by placing telephones in scenes just before "scary encounters".

items.title: Games industry veterans have formed the **Sustainable Games Alliance**, a non-profit organisation dedicated to "helping the games industry become sustainable."
bullets: Backed by noted industry professionals such as Unity Technologies founder David Helgason and Remedy co-founder Petri Järvilehto, Sustainable Games Alliance – which is overseen by managing director and co-founder Maria Wagner – is working to make games "the most sustainable form of entertainment on the planet" by creating actionable ways to help the industry measure, understand, and reduce its footprint.

To achieve this, the Alliance is developing a free, open, and global industry standard for sustainability reporting. It hopes to simplify compliance with key regulations such as the European Sustainability Reporting Standards (ESRS) and the EU’s Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive (CSRD).

items.title: **Owlcat Games** expands into publishing
bullets: "We have already created our dream game, and now we have the expertise to guide others," Owlcat says
Owlcat Games has signed two RPGs by Emotion Spark Studio and Another Angle Games to its new publishing division.
Owlcat has also assumed control of META Publishing, which published Pathfinder: Wrath of the Righteous on PC and console back in 2021.
Owlcat publishing is focused on RPGs and narrative titles. META Publishing is focused on co-op multiplayer games that focus on "gameplay-first"

items.title: **Marvelous USA** and **Xseed Games** to operate as separate publishing labels
bullets: Japanese publisher Marvelous is "taking a more holistic view" to its offerings in the West

Xseed will continue to publish third-party titles including upcoming games The Big Catch and Moonlight Peaks, while Marvelous USA will focus on publishing Marvelous games such as Story of Seasons and the Rune Factor series.
Marvelous USA will also provide its parent company with business, operational, and marketing support to its arcade business unit which is expanding to the West with Naruto Emblem Battle.

items.title: **Keywords** acquires **Wushu Studios**
bullets: The Liverpool-based studio was founded in 2017 by Alan McDermott and provides work-for-hire services to publishers and developers in addition to porting, remaster, and live support services.
Wushu has worked on games including Baldur's Gate 3, Fall Guys, Forza Horizon 5, The Ascent, and State of Decay 2.
The sum of the deal has not been made public

items.title: **Ethical Games** looking to craft industrywide ethics code, seeks developer input
bullets: The game industry lacks a solid code of ethics, and the organization hopes to create one with long-term benefits for developers and players alike.
